title: New pilot program is helping lower accidents involving deer
summary: Syracuse launched a deer management pilot program in 2025 using traps and wildlife officers to reduce vehicle-deer collisions, property damage, and Lyme disease transmission. The program removed 137 white-tailed deer and generated 12,000+ meals for the food bank through venison donation, demonstrating co-benefits of public safety and food security initiatives.
department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

political_signal.description: Pilot program demonstrates multi-benefit approach to urban wildlife management addressing public safety (vehicle accidents), public health (Lyme disease), environmental stewardship (property damage), and food security simultaneously—signals innovation-forward governance.

processing_notes: Article focuses on outcomes rather than governance mechanics; limited process detail available for CIO evaluation
